question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, what must an intermediary do upon cancellation of its certificate of registration?

answer.text: Make provisions for any liability incurred or assumed
Return the certificate to SEBI and cease carrying on any registered activity
Transfer activities to another registered person and allow clients to withdraw securities/funds

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Upon cancellation, the intermediary must: return the certificate, cease all registered activities, transfer activities to another registered person, allow clients to withdraw, make provisions for liabilities, and take other required actions regarding records and investor funds. **The Trap:** Each individual obligation is part of the complete set of requirements upon cancellation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Post-cancellation obligations are tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cancellation does not end obligations - the intermediary must ensure smooth transition for all clients and investors.
